Title: Regulation of volatile benzenoid biosynthesis in petunia flowers

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated when methyl benzoate production starts; rhythmic in petals preceding benzenoid emission
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports volatile benzenoid production by increasing SAM supply for methylation reactions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAM synthetase transcripts are upregulated when methyl benzoate production starts. 2. This induction is absent when ODO1 is suppressed. 3. The gene is implicated in coordinated support of benzenoid synthesis through SAM provision rather than direct catalytic formation of benzenoids.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted transcriptomics; expression comparison in ODO1-suppressed plants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: rhythmic diel emission; ethylene/senescence context discussed for methylation status
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reviewed as part of the SAM cycle; steady-state SAM levels were reported as not strongly altered after ODO1 suppression.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated when methyl benzoate production starts; rhythmic in petals preceding benzenoid emission
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports volatile benzenoid production through SAM-cycle activation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Methionine synthase is transcriptionally upregulated as benzenoid production begins. 2. This induction is lost in ODO1-suppressed plants. 3. The gene is part of coordinated metabolic support for scent biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted transcriptomics; expression comparison in ODO1-suppressed plants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reviewed as supportive SAM-cycle gene rather than direct benzenoid biosynthetic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated when methyl benzoate production starts; rhythmic in petals preceding benzenoid emission
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports volatile benzenoid production by maintaining SAM-cycle turnover
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. S-adenosylhomocysteine hydrolase expression rises with the onset of benzenoid production. 2. This transcriptional upregulation is absent in ODO1-suppressed flowers. 3. The gene is implicated in coordinated methyl donor recycling during scent production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted transcriptomics; expression comparison in ODO1-suppressed plants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review identifies SAM-cycle genes as one of the main discovered gene classes tied to benzenoid emission.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates benzoic acid to methyl benzoate and salicylic acid to methyl salicylate using SAM as methyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in corollas/petals; cyclic/rhythmic expression preceding evening emission; nearly undetectable during pollination-induced senescence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes methyl benzoate synthesis and emission; RNAi knockdown causes ~99% reduction in methyl benzoate emission
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BSMT catalyzes the terminal methylation step forming methyl benzoate, a major petunia floral volatile. 2. Its expression is cyclic and tightly downregulated by ethylene during pollination-induced senescence. 3. BSMT RNAi flowers with 99% reduced methyl benzoate emission are perceptibly altered in scent to humans.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not likely rate limiting according to the review despite high functional importance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene characterization; transcript profiling; ethylene treatment; RNAi knockout; volatile emission analysis; enzyme activity measurements
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light/dark conditions; pollination-induced ethylene; floral senescence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduced sweet scent desirability in human panel when methyl benzoate emission is strongly decreased
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BSMT transcripts can comprise almost 1% of total corolla mRNA during peak synthesis; expression is also high in W138 petals despite low methyl benzoate emission, implying upstream flux limitation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful terminal-step gene for altering scent profile, but alone may not be sufficient to enhance emission because it is unlikely rate limiting

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers benzoyl group from benzoyl-CoA to benzyl alcohol or phenylethanol to form benzyl benzoate or phenylethyl benzoate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: biosynthetic gene upregulated preceding benzenoid emission; downregulated by ethylene during senescence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes synthesis of benzyl benzoate and related benzenoid esters
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BPBT was identified and characterized as a benzenoid biosynthesis gene in petunia flowers. 2. Ethylene downregulates BPBT transcription during senescence. 3. BPBT expression increases when benzenoid production is induced and is upregulated when ODO1 is suppressed, suggesting distinct control from upstream flux regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted transcriptomics; gene characterization; ethylene regulation analysis; expression analysis in ODO1-suppressed plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pollination-induced ethylene; floral senescence; diel rhythmic scent production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Suppression of ODO1 causes BPBT upregulation, suggesting compensatory or precursor-responsive regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate to modify ester composition of floral scent, likely in combination with upstream flux genes

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry from phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for benzenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: rhythmic in petals; transcript levels increase at night concurrent with benzenoid emission; downregulated by ethylene during senescence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports benzenoid synthesis by supplying trans-cinnamic acid precursor; reduced under ODO1 suppression and ethylene treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL converts phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, the precursor for most petunia volatile benzenoids. 2. PAL expression is reduced in ODO1-suppressed plants and by ethylene during senescence. 3. Night-time increases in PAL transcripts coincide with increased trans-cinnamic acid accumulation and scent emission.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supplies a rate-limiting substrate pool together with benzoic acid according to review context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: isotope labeling/metabolic flux context; transcript profiling; ODO1 suppression; ethylene regulation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: High phenylalanine does not alter PAL transcript/activity in petunia petals despite trans-cinnamic acid accumulation, indicating petal-specific regulation differing from other species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important upstream flux control point for engineering increased benzenoid output, especially with other pathway genes

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for phenylalanine-derived benzenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form DAHP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: rhythmic expression in petals similar to benzenoid emission; reduced when ODO1 is downregulated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports benzenoid synthesis by increasing shikimate pathway flux toward aromatic amino acid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAHP synthase is one of the shikimate pathway genes showing rhythmic petal expression associated with scent emission. 2. Its transcript level is strongly reduced when ODO1 is suppressed. 3. The gene contributes to upstream carbon flux into phenylalanine and benzenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key upstream control point implied, not directly established as rate limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted transcriptomics; expression analysis in ODO1-suppressed plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in the review as part of the ODO1-controlled floral shikimate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising upstream flux gene for boosting benzenoid production in combination with regulators

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for phenylalanine-derived benzenoids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in petals, low in leaves; in Mitchell expressed in all three petal cell layers plus vascular tissue; rhythmic like benzenoid emission
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports floral benzenoid biosynthesis by enhancing shikimate pathway flux; expression is activated by ODO1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EPSPS is highly expressed in petals but barely detectable in leaves. 2. ODO1 can activate the EPSPS promoter in leaves, indicating direct or near-direct transcriptional control. 3. EPSPS transcript abundance follows a rhythm associated with benzenoid emission.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key controlled shikimate-pathway step, rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis; promoter studies; transactivation by ODO1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A zinc-finger protein binds the EPSPS promoter and is coexpressed in petals, but its regulatory role was not established.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ODO1; unidentified zinc-finger protein binding EPSPS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful upstream flux target to increase aromatic precursor supply

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: chorismate to prephenate branch toward Phe/Tyr
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch point/first committed step toward Phe and Tyr
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for phenylalanine-derived benzenoids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: rhythmic expression in petals; reduced when ODO1 is suppressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes benzenoid biosynthesis by directing chorismate flux toward phenylalanine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CM expression follows the rhythmic pattern of benzenoid emission in petals. 2. ODO1 suppression strongly reduces CM expression. 3. CM functions as an important branch-point enzyme supplying Phe/Tyr precursors for scent formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch-point control step for aromatic amino acid allocation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted transcriptomics; expression analysis in ODO1-suppressed plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review notes CM as the first committed step in Phe/Tyr biosynthesis and subject to amino-acid feedback inhibition in plants generally.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for engineering upstream precursor partitioning toward benzenoid synthesis

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of shikimate pathway, benzenoid biosynthesis, and SAM-cycle support for volatile benzenoid production
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: EPSPS; DAHP synthase; CM; PAL; SAM synthetase; methionine synthase; S-adenosylhomocysteine hydrolase
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates EPSPS promoter; promotes expression of DAHP synthase, EPSPS, CM and PAL; required for induction of SAM-cycle genes; suppression causes upregulation of BSMT and BPBT
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expression peaks during the night when volatile benzenoid emission is highest and is low the next morning; increase precedes benzenoid emission
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: major positive regulator of floral benzenoid production; suppression strongly reduces volatile benzenoid emission and lowers benzoic acid accumulation up to 20-fold
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulatory factor controlling upstream precursor supply for scent biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ODO1 is an R2R3-MYB-like transcription factor identified as a key regulator of floral benzenoid biosynthesis in petunia. 2. ODO1 expression is rhythmic and precedes volatile emission. 3. Downregulation of ODO1 strongly reduces benzenoid emission by lowering expression of shikimate-pathway and PAL genes and drastically reducing benzoic acid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted transcriptomics; transgenic downregulation/knockout; promoter activation assay; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: diel/circadian-associated rhythmic control; light/energy status implicated indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: evening/night emission rhythm; light/dark conditions relevant to rhythmic scent production
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: suppression greatly reduces floral scent output
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely corresponds to scent locus SCE1; poorly expressed in non-fragrant/low-fragrance W138. Review suggests ODO1 may be more useful than terminal enzymes for engineering scent.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value engineering target and likely superior to terminal enzymes for enhancing floral scent through coordinated flux activation
